title: UnstableSMPDeaths
description: Aims to show Death Messages only to nearby players.
body:
![thumbnail](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/a10a8f9b30e049afc1929a66faba2fd10d63631a.png)

## LocalDeathMessages

**LocalDeathMessages** makes death messages immersive and proximity-based.  
Instead of broadcasting deaths globally, only players close to the death location will see the message.

When a player dies, a configurable sound effect plays for nearby players, adding tension and atmosphere to every death. This makes deaths feel local, personal, and meaningful rather than server-wide spam.

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/Jiq1DbEdLyo" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

---

## Features

- Proximity-based death messages (no global death spam)
- Configurable radius for message visibility
- Optional same-world-only death messages
- Customizable message prefix with color support
- Death sound effects for nearby players
- Option to play the sound to the dying player
- Fully configurable via `config.yml`
- Lightweight and performance-friendly

---

## Configuration

All features can be configured easily in `config.yml`, including:
- Message radius
- World restrictions
- Message formatting
- Sound type, volume, and pitch
- Debug mode

No commands or permissions required — install and it just works.

---

## Compatibility

- Tested on **Paper 1.21.11**
- Supports **Minecraft 1.20 – 1.21.11+**
- Requires **Java 17 or newer**

---

## Support

- Actively maintained by **HeroicStudios**
- Support available via **Discord**
- Bug reports and suggestions are welcome
